events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the compliance determination directly to `/app/answer.json` as requested.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "cat > /app/answer.json <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [
    \"installed-defective\",
    \"non-compliant\"
  ],
  \"percent_complete\": 85,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.94,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Receptacle polarity is reversed, indicating hot and grounded conductors are landed incorrectly.\",
    \"Reversed polarity creates a shock hazard and can leave connected equipment energized in an unsafe manner.\",
    \"Condition is not acceptable for rough-complete electrical work and must not be closed in or accepted.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"De-energize the affected circuit before rework.\",
    \"Correct conductor terminations so the ungrounded conductor lands on the hot terminal and the grounded conductor lands on the neutral terminal.\",
    \"Verify grounding, GFCI protection where required, device rating, enclosure conditions, and polarity with an approved tester before closeout.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"Residential receptacles must be installed with correct polarity and required protection. A reversed-polarity condition is an installed defect and a code compliance failure because it creates an unsafe energized condition and can compromise the intended operation of receptacles and protective devices.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"NEC 210.8\",
    \"NEC 406.4\",
    \"NEC 406.9\"
  ]
}
EOF"
